pattern: Enumerate all 6 (Y-value, non-Y-value) pairs and pick the combination requiring the fewest changes.
hints: First determine which cells belong to the Y shape: the two diagonals converging at the center, and the vertical segment below the center.
Count how often each digit (0, 1, 2) appears in Y-cells and non-Y-cells separately; the cost of assigning value v to Y is (Y-cell count) minus (Y-cells already equal to v).
Try all 6 ordered pairs (y_val, bg_val) with y_val != bg_val and return the minimum total cost.

edge_inputs.description: smallest 3x3 grid, already forms Y with value 1 and bg 0
center cell belongs to both diagonals and vertical - avoid double-counting
5x5 mixed where best choice is y=2 bg=0

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size if size % 2 == 1 else size - 1
    if n < 3: n = 3
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 2) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
